signs: Roots circling the inside of the pot or becoming pot-bound
Water draining straight through with little absorption
Slowed growth during the active season

maintenance.pruning: No routine pruning is required. If stems become too long, leggy or bare, cut them back at the desired height in spring; new leaf clusters will sprout below the cut, and the removed cane sections can be used for propagation. Remove brown or yellowing lower leaves as they age.

nativeClimate.description: Native to tropical Africa, where it grows as an understory plant in warm, humid, frost-free forest and woodland habitats with filtered light. It can reach 15 feet or more in the wild. It is sensitive to cold and to fluoride in water/soil, which causes leaf-tip browning.
